mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity-sensitive retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the RSAFS trend is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 378706 as of one month before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward instances or if its Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above a high threshold signals deteriorating labor income and weaker future consumption, causing investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.5% and rises by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 9.8 as of one month before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if unemployment-threshold signals have nonnegative average forward returns after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while CPI continues to rise, real short rates fall and liquidity remains supportive, raising equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL has positive 3-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.19 as of one month before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained gains in industrial production indicate improving manufacturing output and operating leverage, leading analysts to raise cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 93.8961 as of one month before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO momentum fails to deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum, or if the spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a depressed threshold signal weak construction activity, impaired household balance sheets, and poor credit transmission, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 600 thousand annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 519 as of two months before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with a high but non-worsening unemployment rate identifies a recovery phase in which demand improves before labor slack disappears, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS has positive 3-month growth while UNRATE is above 8% and not rising over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 378706 and UNRATE was 9.8 before 2010-12-15
regime_hint: post-recession demand rebound with labor slack
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-UNRATE trigger does not outperform either RSAFS-only or UNRATE-only signals in 20-day forward return hit rate and mean return during walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll employment momentum reduces recession-risk premia and supports aggregate wage income, causing equities to reprice toward higher expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 130539 as of one month before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month positive-crossing events are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the event-study average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI during a period of stagnant payrolls signals margin pressure without sufficient income growth, increasing stagflation risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth is positive while PAYEMS 3-month growth is less than 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 219.146 and PAYEMS was 130539 before 2010-12-15
failure_condition: Reject if this CPIAUCSL-PAYEMS interaction is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if its estimated interaction coefficient is nonnegative.
